category: User Interface (UI) Misrepresentation of Critical Information
description: The Right-To-Left-Override (RTLO) control character (U+202E) can be used to manipulate the display order of text, creating misleading or deceptive visual representations in source code. Malicious actors can exploit this to: - Mask malicious logic or misrepresent code intent. - Introduce security-critical bugs that are difficult to detect visually. - Deceive auditors, developers, or end-users by obscuring real functionality.
name: Right-To-Left-Override Control Character (U+202E)
remediation: - **Disallow U+202E in source code:** Ensure RTLO and similar Unicode direction control characters are explicitly banned in smart contracts. - **Static analysis tools:** Use static analysis to detect and prevent Unicode control characters in contract source code. - **Education and awareness:** Educate developers about the risks associated with Unicode control characters in smart contract development.
